title: Stagger direction analysis API requests to avoid rate limiting

plan.summary: The `analyzeDirections` function currently fires 8 `fetchWeather` calls simultaneously via `Promise.all`, each of which makes 2 Open-Meteo API calls (weather + marine) — 16 concurrent requests. This consistently triggers Open-Meteo's burst rate limit (429), causing the compass rose to show blank. The fix is to stagger the 8 requests into smaller batches with a short delay between batches, keeping the total time reasonable while staying under the rate limit. I'll batch 2 directions at a time (4 API calls per batch) with a 250ms pause between batches, for a total of ~1s extra latency — a worthwhile tradeoff for reliability.

steps.description: Update the `analyzeDirections` function to replace `Promise.all` for all 8 fetches with a batched approach: process 2 directions at a time using `Promise.all` for each batch, with a 250ms delay between batches. This keeps the total added latency to ~1s while limiting concurrent API calls to 4 (2 weather + 2 marine). The rest of the scoring/hourly logic stays unchanged.
Add a config value `DIRECTION_BATCH_SIZE` (default 2) to control how many directions are fetched in parallel per batch, making it tunable without code changes.

Update the `testDirectionsHourlyStructure` test to verify the staggered approach still returns correct data, and add a new test `testDirectionsBatchConfig` to verify the batch size config is respected.
Run all tests and verify test coverage.
